instruction:
The following table provides the starting players of a basketball team and their heights
$\begin{array}{cccccc}\hline Player & A & B & C & D & E \\ \hline Height (in.) & 75 & 77 & 79 & 82 & 87 \\ \hline \end{array}$
a. The population mean height of the five players is [ANS]. b. Find the sample means for samples of size 2. A, B: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. A, C: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. A, D: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. A, E: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. B, C: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. B, D: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. B, E: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. C, D: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. C, E: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. D, E: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. c. Find the mean of all sample means from above: $\bar{x}$=[ANS]. The answers from parts
(a) and (c) [ANS] A. should always be equal  B. are not equal  C. if they are equal it is only a coincidence.
